author: matkt0
content: Yes, in Besu, we're seeing better results with read. We haven't tested the possibility of linking the read to the transaction. I remember discussions about that. <@792404665068158998>

author: matkt0
content: we are the dicussions regarding that ? it could be a really good improvement

author: nero_eth
content:
Yeah we discussed that but decided against after reviewing the benchmarks gathered in svalbard.
What results do you see for those two tests: `test_single_opcode.py__test_sload_bloated_prefetch_miss[1GB-fork_Amsterdam-benchmark_test-existing_slots_False-distinct_senders_False-benchmark_*M]` and `test_block_access_lists_max_sloads.py__test_bal_max_sloads[fork_Amsterdam-benchmark_test-forward-benchmark_*M]`?

author: matkt0
content:
How do you prevent prefetching? Because if they're not in the BAL, we'll get BAL mismatches. or do you mean prefetch of missing keys in the database ?

What is the name of the test suites with this tests? We can't find them in benchmarkoor or in our local environment.

author: nero_eth
content: one tests includes the bal items in revers order in which they are accesses, this is the worst-case. The other is the happy case

author: nero_eth
content: let met forward that to <@508125616940515329> and <@131476523050860545> who may know why you don't see those tests.

author: nero_eth
content:
If there's be huge differences between reverse and forward and this turns out to be a state-access worst-case, then changing it would be the way to go.
Last time we checked in svalbard this wasn't the case. If things change across clients we could change that in hegota, adding the indices to reads but I wouldn't change anything for glam now.
